arguments.task: Research Airtable using official first-party pages. Visit Airtable developer docs, authentication docs, Web API docs, rate limit docs, pricing or access pages, changelog if relevant, GitHub or MCP registry pages if official or clearly attributable. Extract evidence for: one-line product description, auth methods supported for API access, whether access is self-serve or gated and any pricing/access constraints, API surface type (REST/GraphQL/Both/None) and breadth, whether an MCP server exists, buildability today, and rate limit notes. Return exact supporting URLs and concise supporting excerpts.

input.thought: Start browser-based research on Airtable official sources and MCP evidence.

raw_result.description: Airtable provides a Web API for interacting with Airtable bases, tables, views, and records.
raw_result.auth_notes: Official Web API auth docs describe OAuth for integrations and Personal Access Tokens for user/service access; legacy API keys are deprecated as of February 2024, so token-based auth is the current non-OAuth method.
raw_result.gating_reason: Public Web API documentation is openly available and developers can self-serve with Personal Access Tokens or OAuth app setup; some advanced enterprise/org endpoints require Enterprise Scale, but initial toolkit development is not sales-gated.

api_surface.breadth: Documented Web API covers common data operations across bases, tables, views, records, comments, collaborators, webhooks, metadata, and some admin/enterprise areas, with plan-based availability differences.
api_surface.mcp_notes: No official Airtable MCP server was verified from the gathered evidence. Browser research attempted MCP verification but did not reach a confirmed first-party MCP source, so this is treated as unverified and needs human review.

raw_result.rate_limit_notes: Official rate limits page indicates 5 requests/second per base and 50 requests/second per user or service account token; exceeding limits triggers temporary throttling and clients should use backoff/retry behavior.
raw_result.notable_signals: Official docs show active API maintenance, including authentication migration away from legacy API keys and a changelog page. Billing plans page indicates many endpoints are available on self-serve plans, while certain enterprise/org capabilities are gated to Enterprise Scale.
raw_result.human_review_reason: MCP existence/absence was not conclusively verified from a first-party or authoritative registry source in the gathered evidence. Plan-based endpoint breadth may also need a quick human pass if downstream requires precise endpoint-by-plan mapping.

Billing plans page indicates many endpoints are available on self-serve plans, while certain enterprise/org capabilities are gated to Enterprise Scale.
